systemInstructions: You are an expert Three.js developer. When given a prompt, you will use your creativity and coding skills to create a Three.js scene that perfectly satisfies the prompt. Always return a full HTML document with the Three.js library included. Import the library and any other necessary libraries via the esm.run CDN (e.g. https://esm.run/three). The HTML page should only have a fullscreen canvas element that always resizes to the window size. Remember to set the renderer.setPixelRatio to 2. Always add orbit controls to the scene so the user can rotate the camera. Never attempt to import external assets like models, textures, or shaders, they will not work. Break down object requests into simple primitive shapes. Render them as wireframes. Auto-rotate the camera around the objects. Always use a pure black background. Return ONLY the full HTML page, nothing else, no commentary.

0c5527b3-20a6-457f-b95b-c5037e3a81e9.srcCode:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <title>Deforming Circle</title>
    <style>
        body { margin: 0; overflow: hidden; background-color: #000; }
        canvas { display: block; width: 100vw; height: 100vh; }
    </style>
</head>
<body>
    <script type="module">
        import * as THREE from 'https://esm.run/three';
        import { OrbitControls } from 'https://esm.run/three/examples/jsm/controls/OrbitControls.js';

        // Scene Setup
        const scene = new THREE.Scene();
        scene.background = new THREE.Color(0x000000);

        const camera = new THREE.PerspectiveCamera(75, window.innerWidth / window.innerHeight, 0.1, 1000);
        camera.position.z = 25;

        const renderer = new THREE.WebGLRenderer({ antialias: true });
        renderer.setPixelRatio(2);
        renderer.setSize(window.innerWidth, window.innerHeight);
        document.body.appendChild(renderer.domElement);

        const controls = new OrbitControls(camera, renderer.domElement);
        controls.enableDamping = true;
        controls.autoRotate = true;
        controls.autoRotateSpeed = 2.0;

        // Object Creation: High Detail Sphere (Icosahedron)
        const geometry = new THREE.IcosahedronGeometry(10, 5); // Radius 10, Detail 5 for smooth deformation
        const material = new THREE.MeshBasicMaterial({ 
            color: 0x00ffff, 
            wireframe: true,
            transparent: true,
            opacity: 0.8
        });
        const sphere = new THREE.Mesh(geometry, material);
        scene.add(sphere);

        // Second Object: Inner Core for contrast
        const innerGeo = new THREE.IcosahedronGeometry(5, 2);
        const innerMat = new THREE.MeshBasicMaterial({ 
            color: 0xff00ff, 
            wireframe: true,
            transparent: true,
            opacity: 0.5
        });
        const innerSphere = new THREE.Mesh(innerGeo, innerMat);
        scene.add(innerSphere);

        // Store original positions for calculation
        const originalPositions = geometry.attributes.position.array.slice();
        const innerOriginalPositions = innerGeo.attributes.position.array.slice();

        // Animation Variables
        let time = 0;

        function animate() {
            requestAnimationFrame(animate);

            time += 0.015;

            // Deform Outer Sphere
            const positions = sphere.geometry.attributes.position;
            const count = positions.count;

            for (let i = 0; i < count; i++) {
                // Get original coordinates
                const ox = originalPositions[i * 3];
                const oy = originalPositions[i * 3 + 1];
                const oz = originalPositions[i * 3 + 2];

                // Create a noise-like wave pattern using sine/cosine superposition
                // We normalize the vector to deform along the normal (radius)
                const len = Math.sqrt(ox * ox + oy * oy + oz * oz);
                const nx = ox / len;
                const ny = oy / len;
                const nz = oz / len;

                // Complex wave function
                const wave1 = Math.sin(ox * 0.5 + time * 2);
                const wave2 = Math.cos(oy * 0.3 + time * 1.5);
                const wave3 = Math.sin(oz * 0.5 + time);
                
                // Introduce a "spike" effect
                const spike = Math.sin(time * 3 + ox * 2 + oy) * Math.cos(oz * 2);

                const distortion = 1 + (wave1 * wave2 * wave3) * 0.3 + spike * 0.1;

                positions.setXYZ(
                    i,
                    ox * distortion,
                    oy * distortion,
                    oz * distortion
                );
            }
            positions.needsUpdate = true;

            // Deform Inner Sphere (Subtle breathing effect)
            const innerPos = innerSphere.geometry.attributes.position;
            const innerCount = innerPos.count;

            for (let i = 0; i < innerCount; i++) {
                const ox = innerOriginalPositions[i * 3];
                const oy = innerOriginalPositions[i * 3 + 1];
                const oz = innerOriginalPositions[i * 3 + 2];

                const pulse = 1 + Math.sin(time * 3 + ox) * 0.1;

                innerPos.setXYZ(i, ox * pulse, oy * pulse, oz * pulse);
            }
            innerPos.needsUpdate = true;

            // Rotate inner sphere counter to outer
            innerSphere.rotation.y -= 0.005;
            innerSphere.rotation.z += 0.005;

            controls.update();
            renderer.render(scene, camera);
        }

        // Handle Resize
        window.addEventListener('resize', () => {
            camera.aspect = window.innerWidth / window.innerHeight;
            camera.updateProjectionMatrix();
            renderer.setSize(window.innerWidth, window.innerHeight);
        });

        animate();
    </script>
</body>
</html>
